message:
ci: release-plz workflow + config (br-frankenpandas-4clx)

Closes br-frankenpandas-4clx step 1: wire release-plz for automated
version-bump + CHANGELOG generation + (maintainer-gated) cargo publish
across the 12-crate workspace.

- release-plz.toml: workspace-level config. dependencies_update +
  git_release_enable + git_tag_enable + changelog_update on; publish
  off by default (safe for 0.1.0 pre-release). commit_parsers map the
  conventional-commit prefixes already in use (feat/fix/perf/refactor/
  docs/test/ci/chore/Revert) to CHANGELOG sections.
- .github/workflows/release-plz.yml: two jobs.
  - release-plz-pr: on every main push, opens/updates a Release PR
    with the version bump + changelog diff.
  - release-plz-release: on the merged Release PR's commit, tags +
    (when CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN is set) publishes in topological order.
  Scoped to Dicklesworthstone org so forks don't accidentally publish.
  Concurrency group prevents overlapping release runs.

Workspace.package version.workspace inheritance (bead step 1) already
landed via br-h8a8. Publish topology (fp-types → fp-columnar → ...)
is resolved automatically by cargo-release inside release-plz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-4bn","title":"Add Series.cumprod conformance test coverage","status":"done","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:50:11.323796531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T06:52:16.612712626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenpandas-4clx","title":"[HIGH] release: 12 crates at 0.1.0 with no publish workflow or semver policy — library is structurally un-releasable","description":"Release / publish workflow + versioning audit.

**Observation.**
- All 12 crates sit at version = "0.1.0" (verified via grep). No crate has been bumped since the workspace was initialized.
- No `publish = false` set anywhere. Cargo's default is `publish = true`, which means any developer running `cargo publish -p fp-types` today would push 0.1.0 to crates.io. A moment later, `cargo publish -p fp-columnar` fails because fp-columnar has `fp-types = { path = "../fp-types" }` and path-dependencies can't be published. The publish ordering is undocumented.
- .github/workflows/ has exactly ONE file: ci.yml. No release.yml, no publish.yml, no tag-driven workflow.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` section per crate — there's a workspace-root CHANGELOG but no per-crate versioning story.
- No semver policy documented. The workspace edition is "2024" (Rust 2024 edition, nightly-only), so MSRV is unpinned — but semver needs a user-facing stability story.

**Consequence.**
- First release is impossible today without manual ceremony: 12 Cargo.toml bumps, dependency-path rewrite to `version = "..."` pinned cross-crate refs, cargo publish each in topological order, tag the commit, update Cargo.lock, repeat across all downstream crates. Any misstep breaks the publish.
- frankenpandas-diic bead (PyO3 bindings) depends on the core crates being publishable — the Python wheel pipeline needs `frankenpandas = "X.Y.Z"` from crates.io.
- Multi-agent swarm adds commits faster than any manual release process can keep up. If release is manual, releases never happen; users stay on git dependencies.
- Security fixes (future CVE patches) can't ship quickly because the pipeline doesn't exist.

**Severity.** HIGH — the product is structurally un-releasable today despite being 3171-tests-green. README markets "Add to your Cargo.toml: frankenpandas = { path = 'crates/frankenpandas' }" which is a path dep, not a released version. Users can't consume the library the way the README implies.

**Fix shape:**

1. Workspace-level versioning:
   - Every crate's Cargo.toml [package] version inherits from workspace via `version.workspace = true`.
   - Single source of truth in root Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version = "0.1.0" → bumped once per release.
2. Semver policy in CONTRIBUTING.md (sibling bead): pre-1.0 = minor bumps may break, patch bumps won't; post-1.0 = strict semver.
3. Cross-crate dep version pinning: [workspace.dependencies] gets one fp-types = { version = "0.1.0", path = "crates/fp-types" } entry each, so published crates carry a versioned dep and path-lookup happens only during local dev.
4. Release workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml`:
   - Trigger: git tag matching `v*`.
   - Steps: validate semver bump matches commit messages, run full CI suite, `cargo publish -p <crate>` in topological order (fp-types → fp-columnar → fp-index → fp-runtime → fp-types → fp-expr → fp-groupby → fp-join → fp-io → fp-conformance → frankenpandas), attach CHANGELOG.md diff to GitHub Release.
5. `cargo-release` (external tool) or `release-plz` (GitHub Action) automates the topological publish. Install in CI; configure via release-plz.toml.
6. Per-crate CHANGELOG files auto-generated by release-plz from conventional-commit prefixes (feat/fix/chore/etc — the prefixes are already in use based on recent git log).

Blast radius:
- 12 Cargo.toml edits (version.workspace = true flip, +1 line each).
- workspace Cargo.toml: add [workspace.package] version, add [workspace.dependencies] entries for each fp-* crate.
- 1 new .github/workflows/release.yml (~100 lines).
- 1 new release-plz.toml (~30 lines).
- 1 new section in CONTRIBUTING.md (covered by sibling CODEOWNERS bead).

Prerequisites:
- br-frankenpandas-36qc (cargo-audit) + br-frankenpandas-0l5r (cross-platform CI) ideally green before 0.1.0 ships — you don't want to publish a 0.1.0 that's untested on Windows.
- br-frankenpandas-1d9y (nightly pin) — MSRV claim can't happen without a pinned toolchain.

Once all prerequisites + this bead land, first release becomes `git tag v0.1.0 && git push --tags`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:47.204682706Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:47.204682706Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["publish","release","review","semver"]}

**Observation.**
- All 12 crates sit at version = "0.1.0" (verified via grep). No crate has been bumped since the workspace was initialized.
- No `publish = false` set anywhere. Cargo's default is `publish = true`, which means any developer running `cargo publish -p fp-types` today would push 0.1.0 to crates.io. A moment later, `cargo publish -p fp-columnar` fails because fp-columnar has `fp-types = { path = "../fp-types" }` and path-dependencies can't be published. The publish ordering is undocumented.
- .github/workflows/ has exactly ONE file: ci.yml. No release.yml, no publish.yml, no tag-driven workflow.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` section per crate — there's a workspace-root CHANGELOG but no per-crate versioning story.
- No semver policy documented. The workspace edition is "2024" (Rust 2024 edition, nightly-only), so MSRV is unpinned — but semver needs a user-facing stability story.

**Consequence.**
- First release is impossible today without manual ceremony: 12 Cargo.toml bumps, dependency-path rewrite to `version = "..."` pinned cross-crate refs, cargo publish each in topological order, tag the commit, update Cargo.lock, repeat across all downstream crates. Any misstep breaks the publish.
- frankenpandas-diic bead (PyO3 bindings) depends on the core crates being publishable — the Python wheel pipeline needs `frankenpandas = "X.Y.Z"` from crates.io.
- Multi-agent swarm adds commits faster than any manual release process can keep up. If release is manual, releases never happen; users stay on git dependencies.
- Security fixes (future CVE patches) can't ship quickly because the pipeline doesn't exist.

**Severity.** HIGH — the product is structurally un-releasable today despite being 3171-tests-green. README markets "Add to your Cargo.toml: frankenpandas = { path = 'crates/frankenpandas' }" which is a path dep, not a released version. Users can't consume the library the way the README implies.

**Fix shape:**

1. Workspace-level versioning:
   - Every crate's Cargo.toml [package] version inherits from workspace via `version.workspace = true`.
   - Single source of truth in root Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version = "0.1.0" → bumped once per release.
2. Semver policy in CONTRIBUTING.md (sibling bead): pre-1.0 = minor bumps may break, patch bumps won't; post-1.0 = strict semver.
3. Cross-crate dep version pinning: [workspace.dependencies] gets one fp-types = { version = "0.1.0", path = "crates/fp-types" } entry each, so published crates carry a versioned dep and path-lookup happens only during local dev.
4. Release workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml`:
   - Trigger: git tag matching `v*`.
   - Steps: validate semver bump matches commit messages, run full CI suite, `cargo publish -p <crate>` in topological order (fp-types → fp-columnar → fp-index → fp-runtime → fp-types → fp-expr → fp-groupby → fp-join → fp-io → fp-conformance → frankenpandas), attach CHANGELOG.md diff to GitHub Release.
5. `cargo-release` (external tool) or `release-plz` (GitHub Action) automates the topological publish. Install in CI; configure via release-plz.toml.
6. Per-crate CHANGELOG files auto-generated by release-plz from conventional-commit prefixes (feat/fix/chore/etc — the prefixes are already in use based on recent git log).

Blast radius:
- 12 Cargo.toml edits (version.workspace = true flip, +1 line each).
- workspace Cargo.toml: add [workspace.package] version, add [workspace.dependencies] entries for each fp-* crate.
- 1 new .github/workflows/release.yml (~100 lines).
- 1 new release-plz.toml (~30 lines).
- 1 new section in CONTRIBUTING.md (covered by sibling CODEOWNERS bead).

- br-frankenpandas-36qc (cargo-audit) + br-frankenpandas-0l5r (cross-platform CI) ideally green before 0.1.0 ships — you don't want to publish a 0.1.0 that's untested on Windows.
- br-frankenpandas-1d9y (nightly pin) — MSRV claim can't happen without a pinned toolchain.

Once all prerequisites + this bead land, first release becomes `git tag v0.1.0 && git push --tags`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:47.204682706Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:07:02.551624939Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:07:02.549092304Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["publish","release","review","semver"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-4hkt","title":"fp-conformance: live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling min|max|count","description":"Add mock-free fp-conformance coverage against the live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling(window).min(), max(), and count(). Use interleaved groups plus missing values so windows prove group-local scoping and null-count semantics. Capture parity through phase2c artifacts instead of fp-frame-only unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-22T05:29:31.198884127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:22:53.966730221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T07:22:53.964890760Z","close_reason":"shipped 3269824: 2 fp-frame tests (groupby_rolling_min_max_count_int64_input_widens_to_float, groupby_rolling_window_one_emits_per_row_identity) complementary to x5oy's Float64 window=2 baseline. Locks Int64->Float64 dtype widening on rolling aggregations + window=1 degenerate identity invariant. Clippy clean; 8/8 groupby_rolling tests green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}

@@ -0,0 +1,95 @@
+name: release-plz
+
+# Per br-frankenpandas-4clx: release automation via release-plz.
+# Opens (and keeps updating) a "Release PR" bumping workspace.package
+# version + per-crate CHANGELOG sections based on conventional-commit
+# prefixes. Merging that PR triggers the release job which tags + (when
+# maintainer enables) publishes crates in topological order.
+#
+# Publish is gated by the CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN secret; without it,
+# the release step still tags + pushes GitHub Releases but skips
+# cargo publish. Safe default for pre-0.1.0.
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+
+permissions:
+  pull-requests: write
+  contents: write
+
+concurrency:
+  group: release-plz-${{ github.ref }}
+  cancel-in-progress: false
+
+jobs:
+  release-plz-release:
+    name: Release-plz release
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'Dicklesworthstone' }}
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout repository
+        uses: actions/checkout@v6
+        with:
+          fetch-depth: 0
+
+      - name: Resolve pinned Rust toolchain
+        id: rust_toolchain
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          python3 - <<'PY' >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          import tomllib
+
+          with open("rust-toolchain.toml", "rb") as fh:
+              print(f"channel={tomllib.load(fh)['toolchain']['channel']}")
+          PY
+
+      - name: Setup Rust (pinned nightly)
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
+        with:
+          toolchain: ${{ steps.rust_toolchain.outputs.channel }}
+
+      - name: Run release-plz (release step)
+        uses: release-plz/action@v0.5
+        with:
+          command: release
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
+
+  release-plz-pr:
+    name: Release-plz PR
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'Dicklesworthstone' }}
+    concurrency:
+      group: release-plz-pr-${{ github.ref }}
+      cancel-in-progress: false
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout repository
+        uses: actions/checkout@v6
+        with:
+          fetch-depth: 0
+
+      - name: Resolve pinned Rust toolchain
+        id: rust_toolchain
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          python3 - <<'PY' >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          import tomllib
+
+          with open("rust-toolchain.toml", "rb") as fh:
+              print(f"channel={tomllib.load(fh)['toolchain']['channel']}")
+          PY
+
+      - name: Setup Rust (pinned nightly)
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
+        with:
+          toolchain: ${{ steps.rust_toolchain.outputs.channel }}
+
+      - name: Run release-plz (release-pr step)
+        uses: release-plz/action@v0.5
+        with:
+          command: release-pr
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}

@@ -0,0 +1,39 @@
+# release-plz config for frankenpandas.
+#
+# Per br-frankenpandas-4clx: automate version bumps + changelog entries +
+# cargo-publish in topological order, gated on a release tag.
+#
+# Docs: https://release-plz.ieni.dev/docs/config
+
+[workspace]
+# Single source of truth for version — every crate inherits via
+# `version.workspace = true` in its Cargo.toml (per br-h8a8).
+dependencies_update = true
+git_release_enable = true
+git_tag_enable = true
+# Changelog + PR body derive from conventional-commit prefixes
+# (feat / fix / chore / docs / ci / test / refactor / perf) already in
+# use across the swarm.
+changelog_update = true
+# Don't publish to crates.io in the PR step; maintainer flips
+# publish_timeout + runs a manual release workflow for 0.1.0.
+publish = false
+publish_timeout = "30m"
+# Pre-1.0 semver policy (per CONTRIBUTING.md):
+# breaking changes CAN land in minor bumps; patch bumps never break.
+# release-plz applies this by default when version < 1.0.
+semver_check = true
+
+[changelog]
+# Conventional-commit prefix → section mapping.
+commit_parsers = [
+  { message = "^feat", group = "<!-- 0 -->Added" },
+  { message = "^fix", group = "<!-- 1 -->Fixed" },
+  { message = "^perf", group = "<!-- 2 -->Performance" },
+  { message = "^refactor", group = "<!-- 3 -->Changed" },
+  { message = "^docs", group = "<!-- 4 -->Documentation" },
+  { message = "^test", group = "<!-- 5 -->Testing" },
+  { message = "^ci", group = "<!-- 6 -->CI" },
+  { message = "^chore", skip = true },
+  { message = "^Revert", group = "<!-- 9 -->Reverted" },
+]
